title: Roundcube Webmail Critical Deserialization RCE and SVG XSS Under Active Nation-State Exploitation: CamoFei APT Deploys Godzilla Webshells Against 84,000+ Unpatched Servers (CVE-2025-49113, CVE-2025-68461, CISA KEV)

sources.name: CISA Alert - Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Added to Catalog
Canadian Centre for Cyber Security - Roundcube CVE-2025-49113
TeamT5 - Alert on Exploiting Roundcube CVE-2025-49113
SonarSource - Government Emails at Risk: Critical XSS in Roundcube
ESET Research - Winter Vivern Exploits Roundcube Zero-Day
The Hacker News - Critical 10-Year-Old Roundcube Webmail RCE
The Hacker News - CISA Adds Two Actively Exploited Roundcube Vulnerabilities
SC Media - CISA Adds Critical Roundcube Vulnerabilities to KEV

timeline.event: CVE-2025-49113 deserialization vulnerability introduced in Roundcube codebase version 1.1.0, remaining undetected for over 10 years
CamoFei APT group begins actively exploiting CVE-2025-49113 against educational institutions in Taiwan and government agencies in Pakistan and Myanmar
Roundcube releases security patches 1.5.10 and 1.6.11 fixing CVE-2025-49113 deserialization RCE
Exploit code for CVE-2025-49113 appears for sale on dark web forums within 48 hours of patch release
Public proof-of-concept exploit published, enabling widespread exploitation
Censys identifies 2.4 million exposed Roundcube instances globally with 84,000+ remaining unpatched and vulnerable
Roundcube releases patches 1.5.12 and 1.6.12 fixing CVE-2025-68461 SVG animate XSS vulnerability
CISA adds both CVE-2025-49113 and CVE-2025-68461 to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog with March 13, 2026 federal deadline

simple_summary.what_happened: Two critical vulnerabilities were discovered in Roundcube Webmail, one of the most widely deployed open-source email platforms with over 2.4 million internet-facing instances worldwide. The first flaw (CVE-2025-49113) is a 10-year-old deserialization vulnerability that allows attackers to execute arbitrary commands on the server after obtaining basic login credentials. The second flaw (CVE-2025-68461) is a cross-site scripting vulnerability that lets attackers steal email sessions simply by sending a malicious email containing hidden SVG code. Nation-state threat actors including the CamoFei APT group have been actively deploying webshells and remote access trojans through these vulnerabilities. CISA added both to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on February 20, 2026.
simple_summary.who_is_affected: All organizations running Roundcube Webmail versions prior to 1.5.10 or 1.6.0 through 1.6.10 are vulnerable to the RCE flaw. Versions prior to 1.5.12 or 1.6.0 through 1.6.11 are vulnerable to the XSS flaw. This includes government agencies, universities, ISPs, hosting providers, and any organization self-hosting Roundcube. Over 84,000 vulnerable instances were identified online, with significant concentrations in APAC (4,000 in Japan, 2,500 in Singapore) and across Europe and the Americas.
simple_summary.what_attackers_do: Step 1: For CVE-2025-49113, the attacker obtains valid Roundcube login credentials through credential stuffing, brute force, or prior exploitation of XSS vulnerabilities. Step 2: The attacker sends a crafted request to the upload.php endpoint with a specially constructed _from parameter that corrupts the PHP session by exploiting exclamation mark (!) prefix behavior in session variable names. Step 3: A serialized Crypt_GPG_Engine PHP object is injected into the corrupted session, with the _gpgconf property set to an arbitrary shell command. Step 4: When PHP deserializes the session, the __destruct() method of the injected object calls proc_open() with the attacker's command, achieving remote code execution as the web server user. Step 5: The attacker deploys Godzilla webshells and Pupy RAT for persistent access, then exfiltrates email databases, attachments, and credentials while using the compromised server for lateral movement into internal networks.
what_you_should_do: IMMEDIATE: Identify all Roundcube instances and upgrade to version 1.5.12 or 1.6.12 (which patches BOTH vulnerabilities). Scan using Shodan or Censys with the 'roundcube' filter
If patching is delayed, implement WAF rules to block requests containing '_action=upload' with suspicious _from parameters, and restrict Roundcube access to VPN or trusted IP ranges only

Conduct forensic analysis: Review Apache/Nginx access logs for POST requests to _action=upload endpoints, search web directories for unauthorized PHP files (webshells), and check for unexpected outbound connections from the webmail server
Reset all Roundcube user passwords, revoke API tokens, and implement multi-factor authentication. Block SVG content in emails if not business-critical
simple_summary.why_this_is_serious: Roundcube is the default webmail client for many email hosting platforms and is used by government agencies, educational institutions, and enterprises worldwide. The deserialization vulnerability existed for 10 years before discovery, meaning attackers may have had access to organizations for extended periods. With 84,000+ unpatched instances still online, nation-state groups like CamoFei actively deploying webshells, and the CISA federal deadline of March 13, 2026 approaching in 11 days, this represents an urgent threat to email infrastructure security. Email servers are high-value targets containing sensitive communications, credentials, and attachments.

tactics.description: Attackers exploit internet-facing Roundcube Webmail instances to gain code execution through deserialization vulnerability in upload.php
CVE-2025-68461 enables session hijacking via malicious emails containing SVG animate tags with embedded JavaScript
PHP deserialization triggers automatic execution of injected Crypt_GPG_Engine object's __destruct() method via proc_open()
CamoFei APT deploys Godzilla webshells and custom PHP backdoors in Roundcube plugin directories for persistent server access
Attackers obtain Roundcube credentials through brute force or credential stuffing as a prerequisite for CVE-2025-49113 exploitation
Attackers use compromised access to enumerate mailboxes, exfiltrate email contents, and harvest credentials from email communications
Stolen email data and credentials exfiltrated through deployed webshells and Pupy RAT command-and-control channels

detection_guidance.summary: Detection should focus on identifying exploitation attempts against the upload.php endpoint with suspicious _from parameters, SVG-based XSS payloads in email content, unauthorized PHP files in web directories, and unusual outbound connections from the Roundcube server indicating webshell activity or data exfiltration.

log_sources.description: Monitor for POST requests to Roundcube upload endpoint (_task=settings&_action=upload) with abnormally long _from parameters or serialized PHP object patterns
Watch for session deserialization errors, Crypt_GPG_Engine class instantiation warnings, and unexpected proc_open() execution from the web server process
Alert on web server processes (www-data, apache, nginx) spawning shell processes (/bin/bash, /bin/sh) indicating successful code execution
Monitor Roundcube installation directories for new or modified PHP files that could indicate webshell deployment
Track outbound connections from Roundcube server to external IPs on non-standard ports indicating C2 communication or data exfiltration

what_to_look_for.indicator: POST requests to _action=upload with serialized PHP objects in _from parameter
what_to_look_for.description: The primary exploitation vector for CVE-2025-49113 involves sending crafted requests to the upload endpoint with PHP object serialization patterns (strings starting with O:) in the _from parameter

what_to_look_for.indicator: SVG animate tags in email content
what_to_look_for.description: CVE-2025-68461 exploitation involves emails containing SVG elements with animate tags that execute JavaScript when viewed in Roundcube

what_to_look_for.indicator: New PHP files in Roundcube web directories
what_to_look_for.description: Godzilla webshells and custom backdoors are typically placed in /roundcube/plugins/ or the web root as shell.php, admin.php, test.php, or similar filenames

what_to_look_for.indicator: Web server process spawning shell commands
what_to_look_for.description: Successful deserialization exploitation causes the PHP process to spawn shell commands via proc_open(), visible as www-data or apache user executing /bin/bash
what_to_look_for.log_field: process_monitor: parent='php-fpm' OR parent='apache2' child='/bin/bash' OR child='/bin/sh'

immediate_actions.action: Identify and inventory all Roundcube Webmail instances
immediate_actions.description: Scan internal networks and external attack surface for Roundcube installations. Use Shodan or Censys with 'roundcube' filter. Check web server configurations for Roundcube virtual hosts. Document version, exposure level, and user count for each instance.

These versions patch BOTH CVE-2025-49113 (deserialization RCE) and CVE-2025-68461 (SVG XSS). Back up the database before upgrading. Test in staging if possible but do not delay production patching given active exploitation.

immediate_actions.action: Implement temporary WAF rules if patching is delayed
immediate_actions.description: Block or rate-limit requests to _action=upload endpoints with suspicious _from parameters. Restrict Roundcube access to VPN or trusted IP ranges. Block SVG content in inbound emails at the mail gateway.

immediate_actions.action: Conduct forensic analysis for signs of compromise
immediate_actions.description: Review Apache/Nginx access logs for exploitation signatures against upload.php. Search for unauthorized PHP files in web directories (especially files named shell.php, admin.php, test.php). Check for unexpected outbound connections and cron jobs. Look for Godzilla webshell indicators.

immediate_actions.action: Reset all user credentials and implement enhanced email security
immediate_actions.description: Force password reset for all Roundcube users. Implement MFA if supported. Deploy SPF/DKIM/DMARC. Consider blocking SVG attachments and content in emails. Monitor for unauthorized email forwarding rules.
